subject: A vast subterranean cavern lined with massive stone teeth — stalactites descending from above and stalagmites rising from below, forming a fanged maw of rock around an empty central space
description: A cathedral-scale cave interior dominated by towering stalactites and stalagmites that meet and interlock like fangs in a closing jaw. The chamber is lit from a hidden source somewhere deep within, casting a cool teal-green glow that picks out the wet, mineral-slick surfaces of the rock formations. The foreground edges are nearly black silhouette, framing the receding throat of the cave. No figures are present — the space itself is the subject, vast and predatory in its geometry. The mood is sacred and ominous, a shrine carved by geology rather than hands.
foreground: Dark silhouetted stalactites and stalagmites framing the cave mouth, jagged stone teeth in near-black
background: Deeper recess of the cavern lit by an unseen pale teal-green glow, more rock formations receding into haze
